sections.paragraph:
[unused in Kal], i.q. Arabic كنف to cover, to cover over; whence כָּנָף a covering, a wing. (Comp. the root עוּף.)

Niphal, to cover over oneself, to hide oneself away, Isa 30:20, לֹא־יִבָּנֵף עֹוד מֹורֶיךָ “thy teachers shall no more hide themselves,” i.e. they shall be able to appear in public, without being any more troubled by persecutors. So Abulwalid, and Yarchi; see my Comment. Hence-
